title: Avoid using "volatile" variables with compound operators
description: Compound operators, increments, decrements, and toggling (for booleans) on volatile primitive fields are not atomic operations. This non-atomicity could lead to potential data loss when multiple threads try to update the volatile variable simultaneously. It is recommended to use an atomic class like AtomicInteger, or synchronize the access for safer operations.
recommendation: Replace usage of volatile variables with compound operators with atomic classes like AtomicInteger, or ensure the access is synchronized to prevent potential data loss due to multi-threaded access.
impact: This rule helps prevent data loss in multi-threaded scenarios by warning about unsafe usage of volatile variables with compound operators.

checks.message: Replace usage of volatile variables with compound operators with atomic classes like AtomicInteger, or ensure the access is synchronized to prevent potential data loss due to multi-threaded access.
